questions.question: What is a constitution?
questions.answer: A constitution is a set of fundamental rules that determine how a country is governed and how citizens and government relate to each other.

questions.question: What was apartheid?
questions.answer: Apartheid was a system of racial discrimination practiced in South Africa where non-white people were denied basic rights.

questions.question: Who was Nelson Mandela?
questions.answer: Nelson Mandela was a leader of the anti-apartheid movement and later became the first black President of South Africa.

questions.question: Name the organization that led the struggle against apartheid.
questions.answer: The African National Congress (ANC) led the struggle against apartheid.

questions.question: Why do countries need a constitution?
questions.answer: Countries need a constitution to define government powers, protect citizen rights and maintain order.

questions.question: What does a constitution determine?
questions.answer: A constitution determines the structure of government, powers of institutions and rights of citizens.

questions.question: What is the Constituent Assembly?
questions.answer: The Constituent Assembly was a body of elected representatives that drafted the Constitution of India.

questions.question: When was the Constituent Assembly formed?
questions.answer: The Constituent Assembly was formed in 1946.

questions.question: Who was the chairman of the Drafting Committee?
questions.answer: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the chairman of the Drafting Committee.

questions.question: When was the Constitution of India adopted?
questions.answer: The Constitution of India was adopted on 26 November 1949.

questions.question: When did the Constitution of India come into effect?
questions.answer: The Constitution came into effect on 26 January 1950.

questions.question: Why is 26 January celebrated as Republic Day?
questions.answer: Because the Constitution of India came into effect on that day in 1950.

questions.question: What is the Preamble?
questions.answer: The Preamble is the introductory statement of the Constitution that explains its values and objectives.

questions.question: Which words begin the Preamble of India?
questions.answer: The Preamble begins with the words 'We, the People of India'.

questions.question: What does 'Sovereign' mean?
questions.answer: Sovereign means that India has the power to make its own decisions independently.

questions.question: What does 'Secular' mean?
questions.answer: Secular means the state has no official religion and treats all religions equally.

questions.question: What does 'Republic' mean?
questions.answer: Republic means the head of the state is elected and not a hereditary ruler.

questions.question: What does justice mean in the Constitution?
questions.answer: Justice means fairness and equality for all citizens.

questions.question: What does liberty mean?
questions.answer: Liberty means freedom of thought, expression and belief.

questions.question: What does equality mean in the Constitution?
questions.answer: Equality means that all citizens are equal before the law.

questions.question: What does fraternity mean?
questions.answer: Fraternity means a sense of brotherhood and unity among citizens.

questions.question: How many members were there in the Constituent Assembly?
questions.answer: There were 299 members in the Constituent Assembly of India.

questions.question: What does democracy mean?
questions.answer: Democracy means a system where people elect their rulers and participate in governance.

questions.question: What is a constitutional amendment?
questions.answer: A constitutional amendment is a change made to the Constitution.

questions.question: Why is the Constitution important in a democracy?
questions.answer: It ensures rule of law, protects rights and guides the functioning of government.
